_comment: Documented compat deltas. Each entry suppresses a matching DiffRecord from the active section of summary.md and surfaces it under '## Suppressed (known deltas)' with its reason + regressionTest. See docs/registry/api-compat-deltas.md.

deltas.reason: v3's updateCache is phase-aware: 200 + Git-refresh duration while git-sourced components remain (migration-status git>0), 410 Gone once fully migrated to the DB (git==0, with the Git resolver still parsing components). A db-mode compat candidate is fully migrated (auto-migrate -> git==0) so it returns 410 here; the git-mode candidate (no migration, git>0) returns 200 and matches baseline, so this delta does NOT appear in known-deltas-git.json (empty). baselineValue intentionally omitted: under parallel test load the V1 baseline can transport-fail this heavy operation (Git DSL re-clone) before the HTTP client's timeout — same root cause regardless of whether baseline answers 200 or transport-fails (0). id17 #3666 reproduced the transport-failure variant. Confirmed intentional 2026-05-15; updated for phase-aware 2026-06-01.
deltas.regressionTest: ComponentsRegistryServiceControllerUpdateCacheTest.git equal to zero retires the endpoint with 410 and does not re-read

deltas.reason: Symptom of the STATUS_CODE_DIFF entry above — when the V1 baseline transport-fails the heavy /updateCache call under parallel load, no response headers come back and only candidate (which answers 410 with a JSON ErrorResponse body) reports a content-type. Suppressed together with the status-code deprecation delta.
deltas.regressionTest: ComponentsRegistryServiceControllerUpdateCacheTest.git equal to zero retires the endpoint with 410 and does not re-read

deltas.reason: #256 stale-git bleed-through fix. For a DB-sourced project whose requested version is absent from the DB, the V1/git baseline can still serve a STALE VCSSettings as 200 from legacy DSL, while the v3 candidate routes by resolved component source (ComponentRoutingResolver.resolverForProject) and correctly returns 404. This delta only materialises when the smoke/trace set happens to probe a db-sourced project at a version git still stale-matches; baselineValue omitted because the baseline value is the stale 200 we are intentionally diverging from. The authoritative regression guard is the unit test (this entry is defensive documentation, same convention as the updateCache deltas above).

#256 stale-git bleed-through fix (Distribution counterpart of the vcs-settings entry above). DB-sourced project + version absent from DB: git baseline may serve a stale Distribution as 200; v3 candidate routes by resolved component source and returns 404. Authoritative guard is the unit test; this entry is defensive documentation.

ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): the component-level representative (Component.distribution) is now the OPEN-UPPER (newest) block's value instead of the top-level/oldest block, so docker keys appear (components whose newest range ships docker) and stale GAVs disappear (components whose newest range is docker-only / renamed the artifacts). Domain-owner reviewed every live diff (11 components; keys withheld from this public repo — see the internal compat report, build 4099; re-confirmed 0-active by the 2026-07-05 full re-sweep on v3 HEAD bd31abf4 vs baseline 2.0.88, frozen as crs-compat-baseline v3-full-20260705) and confirmed the NEW values are correct on 2026-07-01. Baseline (old release) still reports the oldest-block values until the amendment ships as the new baseline; then this entry matches nothing.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07) — see the GET /rest/api/1/components STRUCTURAL_DIFF entry.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): the v1 typed list compares the whole collection ONCE, so the 11 amended component-level representatives surface as ONE whole-collection diff. KNOWN BLAST RADIUS (accepted, time-boxed): while this entry is active, AssertJ collapses EVERY v1 list element mismatch into this same single 'components' differ record — so ANY future typed VALUE_DIFF on GET /rest/api/1/components (any component, any field) is also suppressed. Guards that remain live for the suppression window: the raw-layer STRUCTURAL entry above (docker/GAV keys only), the per-component v2 list + v1/v2 detail entries (field-scoped), and the trace-replay raw requests. Self-heals when the amendment ships as the new baseline (record disappears -> entry matches nothing).
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): typed per-element list diffs on the amended component-level fields — distribution.gav/docker (newest block's distribution) and escrow.generation (one component with a historical UNSUPPORTED on the oldest block; newest resolves AUTO). Domain-owner confirmed all 11 affected components 2026-07-01 (keys withheld from this public repo — see the internal compat report, build 4099; re-confirmed 0-active by the 2026-07-05 full re-sweep, crs-compat-baseline v3-full-20260705). Granularity note: one AssertJ record covers ALL differing fields of one component, so a future unrelated field regression CO-OCCURRING on one of the 11 amended components on this endpoint would ride along suppressed until the amendment ships as the new baseline; unaffected components still fail the gate.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): v3 wraps Component, so the single affected component's escrow.generation change (UNSUPPORTED -> AUTO, newest-block escrow) appears under the component.* prefix.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-040 (ADR-018 decoupled): TEST_PER_RANGE_HOTFIX_FORMAT has a bounded merged coverage [1.0,) (its empty [1.0,2.0) block + the [2.0,) override block merge) → exactly one RANGE_PRESENCE row at [1.0,) with NULL overridden_attribute and all typed cols NULL; the base sits at ALL_VERSIONS carrying the OPEN-UPPER [2.0,) hotfix format, and the OLDER [1.0,2.0) block survives as the value-layer override (base = newest value, ADR-018)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): component detail's distribution is the newest block's value — see the GET /rest/api/1/components STRUCTURAL_DIFF entry.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): the standalone component-level distribution endpoint mirrors Component.distribution (newest block). Endpoint has ZERO recorded prod traffic (all real /distribution traffic is per-VERSION) and is deprecated in this release.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): typed standalone-distribution diffs (DistributionDTO fields are top-level here, no distribution.* prefix) — see the STRUCTURAL_DIFF entry above.
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)

deltas.reason: ADR-018 base-row amendment (2026-07): trace-replay raw requests carry LITERAL component-detail paths (the prod-trace replayer does not parameterize), and real component keys must not be committed to this public repo — so this entry matches the path SHAPE (a v1/v2 component-detail request) instead of naming components. Same cause and same field scope (distribution.docker/GAV keys only) as the parameterized GET /rest/api/1/components/{component} STRUCTURAL_DIFF entry; the affected component keys are visible in the internal compat reports (build 4099; 2026-07-05 full re-sweep, crs-compat-baseline v3-full-20260705).
deltas.regressionTest: MigrationIntegrationTest.MIG-036 (ADR-018): TEST_COMPONENT3 base carries the OPEN-UPPER [1.0.107,) release format; the OLDER (,1.0.107) block carries the value-layer overrides (base = newest value)
